payload.answer: I’m not sure how to interpret the shorthand and possible typos in “sam frm goldmn likes bill evns apparently.” Please choose how you want this handled.
repair_options: Treat this as: “Sam from Goldman likes Bill Evans, apparently.”
Treat “goldmn” as Goldman Sachs and store an interaction/preference involving Sam and Bill Evans.
Treat “bill evns” as Bill Evans, the musician, and store that Sam likes Bill Evans.
Treat “likes” as a personal/romantic interest: Sam from Goldman likes Bill Evans.
Treat this as uncertain/rumored only because of “apparently,” and do not store it as a durable fact.
Do not save anything; ask for clarification of who Sam, Goldman/goldmn, and Bill Evans/bill evns refer to.
